_comment: ADO Service Hook configurations for GHAzDO → Teams notifications. Create two service hooks (one per event type) via Project Settings → Service Hooks.

serviceHooks.name: GHAzDO Alert Created → Teams
serviceHooks.description: Sends a Teams notification when a new Advanced Security alert is detected.

serviceHooks.name: GHAzDO Alert State Changed → Teams
serviceHooks.description: Sends a Teams notification when an Advanced Security alert state changes (fixed, dismissed, reopened).

message.text: A new Advanced Security alert has been created.
message.html: A new Advanced Security alert has been created.
message.markdown: A new **Advanced Security** alert has been created.

detailedMessage.text: Advanced Security alert #42: Secret detected in src/config/db.js (critical severity) in repository my-project-repo.
detailedMessage.html: Advanced Security alert #42: Secret detected in src/config/db.js (critical severity) in repository my-project-repo.
detailedMessage.markdown: Advanced Security alert **#42**: Secret detected in `src/config/db.js` (critical severity) in repository **my-project-repo**.

message.text: An Advanced Security alert state has changed.
message.html: An Advanced Security alert state has changed.
message.markdown: An **Advanced Security** alert state has changed.

detailedMessage.text: Advanced Security alert #42 state changed from 'active' to 'fixed' in repository my-project-repo.
detailedMessage.html: Advanced Security alert #42 state changed from active to fixed in repository my-project-repo.
detailedMessage.markdown: Advanced Security alert **#42** state changed from `active` to `fixed` in repository **my-project-repo**.

setupInstructions.step1: Go to Azure DevOps → Project Settings → Service Hooks → + Create subscription
setupInstructions.step2: Select 'Web Hooks' as the service (or 'Microsoft Teams' if available)
setupInstructions.step3: For alert created: Choose event 'Advanced Security alert created'
setupInstructions.step4: For state changed: Choose event 'Advanced Security alert state changed'
setupInstructions.step5: Paste the Teams Incoming Webhook URL in the URL field
setupInstructions.step6: Click Test to verify, then Finish
setupInstructions.note: Replace all {PLACEHOLDER} values with your actual project details
